summary: A solid Charlie Miller sound‑board transfer captures an average ’73 show with standout moments like a blistering Eyes of the World jam, Big River and Me & My Uncle.
review: The recording is a well‑preserved SBD that many reviewers rate highly for mix and transfer quality, though it contains a few tuning edits and minor missing sections. Musically the concert sits in the middle of the 1973 era – rough at the start but redeemed by highlights such as Big River, Me & My Uncle, and an especially adventurous Eyes of the World > Sugar Magnolia sequence.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality averages around three stars (average 1973 performance) while recording quality is praised as good‑to‑excellent; weighted combination yields a mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp Charlie Miller transfer with minor tuning edits and a brief missing segment in the second set; overall mix praised as excellent.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Late start (5‑hour delay) made the first set uneven, but it tightened around Me & My Uncle and Big River. The shortened second set delivers a long, exploratory Eyes of the World jam that flows into Sugar Magnolia before ending.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Weaves his frets through the extended Eyes jam; ideas noted as among the year's best.
band_member_comments.Phil: Steers and drives the band during Eyes of the World, making the crowd vibrate; highlighted as a standout performance.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith is praised as great on keyboards throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill briefly stops during the improv then re‑enters, adding dynamic contrast.
